codeReview.summary: The rerun still does not implement the requested fix. Commit 1269490 only changes the Playwright regression to click Calendar after returning to /timer, while the runtime continues restoring persisted TimerView state instead of landing back on Calendar automatically.

issues.description: The test now clicks the Calendar toggle immediately after returning to `/timer` in workspace B. The feature requires the page itself to land with Calendar active again in the workspace-switch return flow, so this masks the regression instead of fixing it.

The same manual Calendar re-selection is repeated after switching back to workspace A. Because the assertions only pass after an extra click, the commit still does not prove or enforce that `/timer` reopens with Calendar active.

The timer page still initializes view state from `loadPersistedTimerView()` and persists alternate views to localStorage. The reviewed commit does not change this behavior, so returning to `/timer` can still reopen in list or timesheet instead of Calendar, contrary to the feature description and validation contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker explicitly handed off a rerun-only test alignment even though the frontend-test-worker skill requires fix features to point at the commit containing the scoped behavior change, or to say no scoped implementation was needed instead of citing an unrelated green rerun.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff /Users/opentoggl-ci/.factory/missions/e037dfea-351f-4685-b798-ddaaec9dfb1c/handoffs/2026-03-25T03-32-24-006Z__tracking-timer-calendar-return-regression-fix__ee376bf3-e088-4eeb-ad67-7e373ff1ba52.json states that the regression is addressed by the test's explicit Calendar click, not by code change, while still reporting commitId `1269490` as the fix. The skill file .factory/skills/frontend-test-worker/SKILL.md says code-fix handoffs must identify the commit containing the behavior change and not treat rerun-only passes as completion for a flaky/regression fix feature.

summary: Fail: the rerun commit only updates VAL-REG-002 to click Calendar after returning to `/timer`, while the app still restores persisted TimerView state from localStorage. The requested fix—that the workspace-switch return flow lands on `/timer` with Calendar active again—remains unimplemented.
